body:
```
lexeme ru
      surface
        text помидор
      surface
        text помидоры
      surface
        text томат
```

Our meanings are not detailed enough.

What I want to see, is what `помидор`, `помидоры`, `томат` are in relation to meaning, for example is it singular or plural and so on.

And also each words itself should be fully defined (its composition).

So if meanings is reverse dictionary, description of itself is regular direct dictionary.

We need to make much more detailed descriptions of both meanings and words.

And if meaning reference possible ways to express it using words, words should reference possible meanings.

Also it is strange that for `помидор` we have plural, and for `томат` we don't.

So the ideal goal is to allow our system use all the semantics we can collect from other sources.

And our construction should ideally in normalized way in respect to each type of link.

We also need to find actual tasks and questions and other relevant places in the code that will actually benefit of the more detailed knowledge links network structure.

We also need to find all previously hardcoded strings in the code, and keep meanings, and their translation from any language to any language in our data, and that data should be grounded in real data (which for tests we can precache as we already do).

We need to make sure each and every concept in our codebase is fully expressed in meanings.

Also we have a huge portion of code in formal worker in JavaScript. I expected for us to have WebAssembly web worker, and JavaScript should solve only interfacing with the UI, not reimplementing the logic.

We also need to double check we don't repeat ourselves, and if we absolutely must convert some code to JavaScript for logic, it should be compiled at build time from Rust to JavaScript.

So the code on JavaScript should be kept minimal.

Also we should have CST/AST of all our Rust logic (meta algorithm) in our data, so we can use our algorithm to reason about itself, add missing parts to itself (for example in places where we place unhandled error exceptions).

And we should be able to rebuild Rust logic on demand from full CST/AST of it. And also we should have generated mermaid diagram split into parts so we can have high level overview of the whole system algorithm visually by humans.

We should make different mermaid diagram describing in detail what exactly happens when the input to the system coming from different places.

And we also need to make interactive debugging view (that should be separate experimental view, for which we can use embedded VS Code, that is split into different views in a debug view), where while the formal AI is thinking, we are able to execute it step by step showing split view with chat, data, mermaid diagram for the part of algorithm, rust version of the code, javascript version of the code.

So we need to make sure our universal meta algorithm is fully inspectable, and is able to reason about itself.

We need to make sure everything from what I ask here if reflected in our docs, roadmaps, requirements and so on.

We also must double check that we fully apply https://github.com/link-foundation/code-architecture-principles in our system.

Yet the meta algorithm for processing messages, talking, executing tools, solving tasks - the meta algorithm should be fully universal, should reason about itself, should be able to reason about any message, any task, any question.

Any message should be treaded as statement formalized into meta language and weighted using probability of being true given all we know in the context of dialog, in the context of all dialogs and so on. And also when user gives conflicting requirements, we need ways to warn user about this, and propose solutions to paradoxes, contradictions and so on in different ways, dimensions, axis, criteria.

Even this task itself must be solvable by our meta algorithm.

So the best tactic is to use our own Formal AI via agent tool CLI (https://github.com/link-assistant/agent) it must be possible to spawn instance of Formal AI server, and to give him this task. It surely will fail soon, you can connect to it via debugger, or find any other way to iterate.

So the goal is to use our meta algorithm, to reason through it about this task, fixing all the failures on the way, and as the result we should get json file with Agent CLI session that fully solved this exact task, i mean literally https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/538.

So you are the guide for our algorithm, and any missing or wrong reasoning must be fixed using our meta algorithm itself.

So there is a simple rule: you don't read or edit code or files yourself, you only use Agent CLI with Formal AI server connected to do it.

If https://github.com/link-assistant/agent has any blockers, that we cannot do workaround - we can create an issue and pause.

If Formal AI is not able to do changes in files as requested - you go and fix it, until it can, each time you should use different natural language requests, so we test, that solutions are never hardcoded, but truly general.

So it is like a game you go through the task using another intelligence, and when it fails you keep fixing it, using itself.

So you can fallback to reading or editing code yourself, only when proven Agent CLI with Formal AI connected cannot, yet immediately you need to make sure you fix Agent CLI with Formal AI just enough for it to be able to do such tests in general, and retry the step, even if it is already done, yet if formal AI will not react accordingly like you would do or any human - you fix it all, and each small fixing step should also be done with Agent CLI itself.

By doing it all so - we will be able to ensure that our Formal AI is truly and fully general AI system, that is able to do coding, and keep improving, solving tasks in itself by modifying intelligence its data (memory) and meta algorithm (reasoning).

If it is hard to do at once, you can take portions of this task and simplify them and solve part by part. Yet at the end, this task will be fully solved only if we will be able to make a separate copy of the repository without this branch and pull request changes and fully get the same or very close changes in separate test repository operated by Agent CLI. Make sure to run only single repository tests at a time, as Rust cargo cache can take quite a lot of space, and we can quickly run out of space, so manage space wisely.

So you can have playground in temporary folder, and you actual pull request branch in two different folders, so if anything breaks, you will be able to fix it safely.

We also must write to our contributing.md, that from this day and task forward this is the only way how we do the development of our Formal AI system.

Take me very seriously, because as I see now, that is the only way, that will bring us fast and recursive iterations. And we will guarantee for any next task we will use our Formal AI itself to solve them, and in such a way we will learn in our data and meta algorithm everything needed to gather knowledge on demand to solve each and every task, and make our meta algorithm as universal as possible to actually being able to solve all the tasks not by memorizing (it is impossible to memorize any possible solution to any possible task), but by generalizing from our experience on each and every step.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.

Make sure do make as small commits as possible, as this process may fail on each step, so we will have something preserved in the history if everything fails.
title: Make our meanings and words more detailed
